title: Skip Count on a Number Line
essential_question: How can you use a number line to skip count and find how many in all?
strategies: Draw equal jumps on a number line
Each jump = the size of each group
Number of jumps = number of groups

items.question: Skip count by 3s on a number line: 0, 3, 6, 9, ?. What comes next?
Skip count by 5 on a number line. Make 4 jumps. Where do you land?
Louise skip counts by 4 on a number line to find 5 × 4. How many jumps?
Theo needs 4 boards, each 3 feet long. How many feet total? (Skip count by 3.)
Skip count by 6 on a number line. Make 3 jumps. Where do you land?

items.title: Skip Counting on a Number Line
items.content: A number line starts at 0. Each jump is the same size. To find 3 × 4, make 3 jumps of 4: start at 0, jump to 4, then 8, then 12. You land on 12, so 3 × 4 = 12.

items.title: Number of Jumps = Number of Groups
items.content: For 5 × 4: make 5 jumps (groups), each jump is 4. For 4 × 5: make 4 jumps, each is 5. Both land on 20!

items.title: Using the Number Line to Multiply
items.content: Step 1: Start at 0. Step 2: Decide the jump size (= items per group). Step 3: Jump that many times (= number of groups). Step 4: Where you land is the product.

items.question: Skip count by 2 on a number line. Make 6 jumps. Where do you land?
